nodes.Description:
Canvas Graph is a keyboard-first visual editor for **writing and navigating structured graphs** that explain systems, concepts, sequences, workflows, and nested maps of memory. A person should be able to open the web app without prior instruction, create nodes and relationships, organize them spatially and hierarchically, keep the work locally, and deliberately hand another person an independent copy through a direct link or portable file. The product is local-first and single-user. Sharing means “send a copy that the recipient can open”; accounts, servers, and real-time collaboration are not part of this mission.
Contract status: Draft for product-owner review · baseline 2026-07-15
Source: requirements/requirements.md · regenerate with npm run requirements:graph
Legend: square = 0.1; circle = Later; “Open blocker” is unresolved release work.
Evidence: 25/212 release Capabilities have accepted proof; 187 remain unproven (187 missing, 0 pending, 0 rejected, 0 stale; 27 records).
Correct, complete, lossless end-to-end behavior: it should work.

Shell
The shell loads the graph stage, registered controls, panels, modal place, and notice place as one usable application.
Evidence: missing · no accepted proof record

Shell
Canvas pan, zoom, layout, selection, and history never translate, clip, or move shell chrome away from its viewport anchors.
Evidence: missing · no accepted proof record

Graph documents
A user can create a new empty graph without changing existing graphs.
Evidence: missing · no accepted proof record

Graph documents
A user can switch among graphs and always see which named graph is active.
Evidence: missing · no accepted proof record

A user can rename the active graph and the new name is used by navigation and persistence.

Discoverable, clear, convenient, visually coherent, focused, and reachable in at most three interactions; keyboard-first on desktop, touch-friendly on mobile, with UI/palette parity and genuinely hideable clutter.

requirementsMap.encoding: Open root → folded Attribute containers → Component sections → Capability nodes
requirementsMap.legend: Square = 0.1; circle = Later; definition card = Attribute meaning; blocker text = unresolved release work

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#maps every Capability into one Attribute container and Component section
evidenceRecords.notes: The generator validates and deterministically projects the complete requirements model.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#imports as a rendered Canvas Graph document with Attribute containers
evidenceRecords.notes: The root opens, nested Attributes start folded, and opening a sibling preserves the exclusive reading policy.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#imports as a rendered Canvas Graph document with Attribute containers
evidenceRecords.notes: Generated requirements reject ordinary graph mutation commands.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#searches human Capability prose and opens a result at readable scale
evidenceRecords.notes: Navigator search indexes meaningful Capability prose.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#searches human Capability prose and opens a result at readable scale
evidenceRecords.notes: A search result reveals its ancestor and reaches a readable camera scale.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/ui-layout-pass.test.ts#reserves readable space for long edge labels
evidenceRecords.notes: Layout spacing budgets long relationship labels.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/ui-layout-pass.test.ts#centres content in the canvas area not covered by the navigator
evidenceRecords.notes: Requirements fitting uses the unobscured reading frame.
Add a real-layout assertion that every generated card exposes its complete normative prose without scroll or clipping.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#maps every Capability into one Attribute container and Component section
evidenceRecords.notes: Evidence records validate and generation derives per-Capability readiness state and aggregate coverage.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#filters the review index by accepted evidence, Attribute, and Component without flattening hierarchy
evidenceRecords.notes: Review filters compose while retaining Attribute grouping and human status labels.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/io-persistence.test.ts#restores multiple graphs and the active graph id
evidenceRecords.notes: A reload restores multiple complete graphs, the active graph ID, its name, and its document contents.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#searches and top-aligns Component sections as jumpable navigator results
evidenceRecords.notes: Component sections are individually searchable, unfold their Attribute path, select their container, and align to the top reading edge.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/container-commands.test.ts#unfolds a collapsed container on double-click with maximize and minimize icons
evidenceRecords.notes: Double-click unfolds a collapsed item and its toolbar icon changes from maximize to minimize.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/view-camera.test.ts#fits a tall document no lower than 80% and lets its lower end continue off-screen
evidenceRecords.notes: Oversized Fit holds the 80% floor, top-aligns the document, and deliberately leaves its lower end off-screen.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/requirements-graph.test.ts#searches ordinary node descriptions in the graph navigator
evidenceRecords.notes: Ordinary graph descriptions participate in navigator search and appear in the matching result.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/node-commands.test.ts#offers fold only for described nodes and clears stale fold state with the description
evidenceRecords.notes: Empty nodes cannot fold, described nodes can, and removing the description clears both the control and stale fold state.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/node-visuals.test.ts#switches node shapes from the panel and renders markdown descriptions
evidenceRecords.notes: Safe Markdown blocks and inline forms render as semantic DOM rather than literal syntax.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/node-visuals.test.ts#switches node shapes from the panel and renders markdown descriptions
evidenceRecords.notes: Editing a Markdown description recalculates the auto-sized node from rendered block geometry.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/release-hardening.spec.js#node, container, and edge selections remain unmistakable in grayscale
evidenceRecords.notes: Strong node/container halos and a heavier edge stroke make selection visible across item kinds.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#lays out vertical, horizontal, tree, and radial grammars deterministically
evidenceRecords.notes: All four release styles produce their distinct spatial grammar.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#lays out vertical, horizontal, tree, and radial grammars deterministically
evidenceRecords.notes: Relationship depth becomes indentation, rows, levels, or distance from a stable radial hub.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#lays out vertical, horizontal, tree, and radial grammars deterministically
evidenceRecords.notes: Reapplying every release layout returns byte-equal node positions.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#uses V/H/T/R as mode shortcuts and exposes the active style as a pressed control
evidenceRecords.notes: Each style has a visible labeled control, pressed state, palette command, and direct shortcut.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#makes A advance a vertical list and Shift+A create its connected right branch
evidenceRecords.notes: The actual keyboard stream creates the requested vertical next-item and nested-child outcomes.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#mirrors primary and branch creation for horizontal, tree, and radial modes
evidenceRecords.notes: Horizontal, Tree, and Radial creation use their documented primary and branch directions.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#keeps each graph layout style in its snapshot and through duplication
evidenceRecords.notes: Active layout is per-graph snapshot data, survives duplication, and restores after graph switching.

evidenceRecords.proof: tests/commands/layout-styles.test.ts#opens the expanded Game graph as a readable vertical nested list
evidenceRecords.notes: The editable 21-node Game example covers four disciplines and sixteen detailed topics without vertical overlap.

openBlockers.condition: The proposed mission, Attributes, Components, and 0.1 markers are not yet owner-approved.
openBlockers.exit: Owner approves this baseline or edits it to approval.

openBlockers.condition: The owner approved the parity rule, but no accepted inventory proves visible-UI/palette parity for every 0.1 capability root.
openBlockers.exit: Add the parity inventory/test and resolve every mismatch.

openBlockers.condition: The repository roadmap identifies pointer-stream helpers for drag and resize as a remaining unified-test gap.
openBlockers.exit: Drive pointer, wheel, resize, and multi-touch journeys through the shared scenario contract with regression coverage.

openBlockers.condition: Real layout checks exist as bespoke Playwright tests, while the roadmap identifies a data-driven layout oracle as missing.
openBlockers.exit: Add scenario-level real-layout assertions and migrate representative layout regressions to them.

openBlockers.condition: Chromium-only is approved, but the exact tested release version and retained desktop/touch CI proof are not yet recorded in public release material.
openBlockers.exit: Record the Chromium version used by the release gate and link its passing desktop and 390 × 844 results from the support statement.

openBlockers.condition: GitHub Pages is approved, but no Pages workflow, base-path proof, or canonical HTTPS URL is configured or documented.
openBlockers.exit: Deploy the release artifact to GitHub Pages and record the canonical URL with passing direct-route and share-link checks.

openBlockers.condition: Production-path smoke testing cannot be completed before the GitHub Pages target exists.
openBlockers.exit: Smoke-test the exact artifact through the Pages release path and retain the result.

openBlockers.condition: “Canvas Graph” is intentionally a working label until the latest safe release step.
openBlockers.exit: Before tagging or production promotion, choose the final name and update every enumerated public surface in one verified change.

openBlockers.condition: Changelog dating, tag, release archive, and checksum are intentionally pending final readiness.
openBlockers.exit: Complete them from the accepted release commit.

openBlockers.condition: Apache-2.0 is selected, but source and built artifacts do not yet contain the required license and attribution material.
openBlockers.exit: Add and verify Apache-2.0 license/notice inclusion in source and distribution.

openBlockers.condition: The evidence ledger and generated per-Capability states now exist, but only 25 of 212 release Capabilities have accepted proof; the remaining 187 are explicitly missing.
openBlockers.exit: Map every 0.1 Capability to accepted automated evidence or a dated manual proof and keep every proof locator valid at the accepted release baseline.
